desc: Liepāja is Latvia's windy culture-and-beach city: part music capital, part white-sand Baltic coast, part military-history outlier, with enough Art Nouveau streets and seaside space to feel very different from Riga. It suits travellers who want a more characterful, rough-edged coastal break than the usual polished old-town weekend.

todo.name: Liepaja beach
todo.desc: Walk the broad sand and dunes, accepting that wind is part of the city's Baltic character.

todo.name: Karosta Prison
todo.desc: Tour the former military prison and nearby Orthodox cathedral for Liepaja's starkest heritage layer.

todo.name: Great Amber concert hall
todo.desc: Check the programme at the amber-coloured concert hall before dates are fixed.

todo.name: Seaside Park
todo.desc: Use the park and promenade for a relaxed link between town and beach.

todo.name: Art Nouveau and old centre walk
todo.desc: Look beyond the beach and Karosta, because the city's streets and façades carry much of its personality.

todo.name: Festival or concert-hall timing check
todo.desc: Liepāja's mood changes a lot with its event calendar, so one cultural booking can make the whole stay click into place.

wifi.note: Usually solid in central stays and cafés, but the destination's real friction is weather and spread rather than connectivity; the coast and Karosta feel bigger and windier than the map suggests.

prac.fltNote: Liepāja is easy by bus, rail or road from Riga, but for many international visitors the more practical commercial air gateway can also be nearby Palanga in Lithuania rather than the city's own small airport. That means this is still a transfer destination in reality, even if the final overland leg is manageable. Summer festivals and European Capital of Culture buildup are the moments when room pressure jumps most sharply.
prac.lang: Latvian; English and Russian may be encountered in visitor services
prac.visa: Schengen rules apply; UK/Irish travellers should verify 90-in-180-day limits and passport validity before travel.
alerts: Wind makes beach days feel cooler than inland Latvia.
Karosta tours and cultural events need schedule checks.
Winter daylight is short for coast-heavy plans.

sweet: June to August is usually Liepāja's best stretch for beach weather, park life and event energy, while late May and early September are often better if you want the coast without the busiest summer rhythm.
